things.name: Schloss Hellenstein
things.description: Impressive Renaissance castle perched on a rocky hilltop above Heidenheim with panoramic views and a museum of local history

things.name: Voith Freilufttheater
things.description: Germany's largest open-air opera theatre set against the dramatic rocky backdrop of Schloss Hellenstein

things.name: Naturpark Schwäbisch-Fränkischer Wald
things.description: Sweeping Swabian-Franconian forest nature park with walking trails, cycling routes and dramatic Jura limestone landscapes

things.name: Voith Museum
things.description: Industrial heritage museum dedicated to the Voith engineering dynasty whose innovations shaped global paper and hydropower industries

things.name: Heidenheim City Centre
things.description: Compact pedestrianised town centre with a bustling weekly market and the historic Rathaus square, proud home of 1. FC Heidenheim

things.name: Römisches Freilichtmuseum Hechingen-Stein
things.description: Open-air Roman villa museum near the Swabian Alb with excavated baths, mosaic floors and reconstructed living quarters

things.name: Brenzpark
things.description: Peaceful riverside park along the Brenz with botanical gardens, paddling areas and walks through the Heidenheim valley

things.name: Ulm Minster
things.description: The world's tallest church steeple at 161 metres rises over Ulm's medieval old town, reachable from Heidenheim in 40 minutes

things.name: Steinheim Meteorite Crater
things.description: Well-preserved 15-million-year-old meteor impact crater near Heidenheim, now a peaceful basin with a nature walk and small museum

things.name: Heimatmuseum Heidenheim
things.description: Local history museum exploring Heidenheim's Swabian heritage, guilds, traditional crafts and industrial development
